provisions.description: Prohibits use of DoD funds under section 101 for: (1) new production of items not funded in FY2023 or prior; (2) increased production rates above FY2023 levels; (3) initiation, resumption, or continuation of any project/activity not available in FY2023; and (4) multi-year procurement advance funding for economic order quantity unless later appropriated.
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 102. (a) No appropriation or funds made available or authority granted pursuant to section 101 for the Department of Defense shall be used for: (1) the new production of items not funded for production in fiscal year
notes: Standard CR limitation on DoD to prevent new starts and production increases
Subsection (b) separately prohibits multi-year procurement advance funding for economic order quantity

provisions.description: Appropriations made by section 101 shall be available to the extent and in the manner that would be provided by the pertinent appropriations Act.
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 103. Appropriations made by section 101 shall be available to the extent and in the manner that would be provided by the pertinent appropriations Act.
notes: Standard CR boilerplate — availability mirrors the underlying FY2023 acts

provisions.description: Except as provided in section 102, no appropriation or funds under section 101 shall be used to initiate or resume any project or activity for which appropriations were not available during FY2023.
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 104. Except as otherwise provided in section 102, no appropriation or funds made available or authority granted pursuant to section 101 shall be used to initiate or resume any project or activity for which appropriati
notes: Standard CR prohibition on new starts — government-wide (not just DoD)

cross_references.target: SEC. 102

provisions.description: Appropriations and authority under this Act shall cover all obligations or expenditures incurred during the period funds are available.
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 105. Appropriations made and authority granted pursuant to this Act shall cover all obligations or expenditures incurred for any project or activity during the period for which funds or authority for such project or ac
notes: Standard CR boilerplate — ensures obligations during the CR period are covered

provisions.description: Sets the CR expiration date at November 17, 2023. Funds are available until the earlier of: (1) enactment of a specific appropriation; (2) enactment of the applicable FY2024 appropriations act without the project; or (3) November 17, 2023.
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 106. Unless otherwise provided for in this Act or in the applicable appropriations Act for fiscal year 2024, appropriations and funds made available and authority granted pursuant to this Act shall be available until w
notes: CR expiration date: November 17, 2023
Section 106(3) is referenced extensively throughout the Act as the end date

provisions.description: Expenditures under this Act shall be charged to the applicable appropriation, fund, or authorization when the regular appropriations bill is enacted.
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 107. Expenditures made pursuant to this Act shall be charged to the applicable appropriation, fund, or authorization whenever a bill in which such applicable appropriation, fund, or authorization is contained is enact
notes: Standard CR boilerplate — charges CR spending against final enacted appropriations

provisions.description: Allows use of funds without regard to apportionment time limitations under 31 U.S.C. 1513, while preserving other apportionment law provisions.
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 108. Appropriations made and funds made available by or authority granted pursuant to this Act may be used without regard to the time limitations for submission and approval of apportionments set forth in section 1513
notes: Waives OMB apportionment timing requirements for CR administration

provisions.description: Prohibits high initial rates of operation or complete distribution of appropriations at the beginning of FY2024 for programs that distribute funds to States, foreign countries, grantees, or others. No grants may be awarded that would impinge on final funding prerogatives.

provisions.description: Requires this Act to be implemented so that only the most limited funding action permitted shall be taken to continue projects and activities.
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 110. This Act shall be implemented so that only the most limited funding action of that permitted in the Act shall be taken in order to provide for continuation of projects and activities.
notes: Standard CR boilerplate — minimum necessary funding principle

notes: Part of Subtitle E — Human Services
Extends program authorization and funding for the period Oct 1 – Nov 17, 2023 on a pro-rata basis of FY2023 level
Adds exception in subsection (c)(3) exempting this period's funds from certain allocation requirements
Subsection (f) provides an amount equal to the pro rata portion of the FY2023 appropriation

provisions.description: Exempts the budgetary effects of Division B from (a) statutory PAYGO scorecards under section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010, (b) Senate PAYGO scorecards maintained for purposes of section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress), and (c) budget scorekeeping classifications under section 251 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, allocations under section 302(a) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974, and paragraph (4)(C) of section 3 of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 2401. 
Budgetary effects
(a) 
Statutory paygo scorecards
The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go
notes: Standard budget-neutrality exemption provision for Division B
Subsection (a) exempts from statutory PAYGO scorecards per the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
Subsection (b) exempts from Senate PAYGO scorecards per H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress)

Subsection (c) exempts from three additional scorekeeping mechanisms: (1) section 251 of Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, (2) section 302(a) allocations of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974, and (3) paragraph (4)(C) of section 3 of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
Notwithstanding Rule 3 of the Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines from Conference Report 105-217 and section 250(c)(8) of BBEDCA

flagged_issues: SEC. 101 contains 10 CR substitutions reducing supplemental amounts from Divisions M and N of P.L. 117-328
SEC. 124 allows $621,270,000 for Columbia Class Submarine notwithstanding DoD new-start limitations
SEC. 126 increases OPM Salaries and Expenses from $190,784,000 to $219,076,000 above FY2023 baseline
SEC. 129 provides $16,000,000,000 emergency supplemental for FEMA Disaster Relief Fund
SEC. 133 contains an indefinite appropriation ('such sums as may be necessary') for Social Security Act activities — not included in total_budget_authority
SEC. 137 contains offsetting substitutions for FAA F&E: +$47M general fund / -$47M trust fund, net zero change
Section 521(d)(1) CR substitution in SEC. 101(2) lacks a named account — likely a CJS general provision (possibly Crime Victims Fund)
SEC. 2101(a)(2) contains an indefinite appropriation (pro rata of FY2023 amounts) — no specific dollar figure extractable.
SEC. 2101(b) extends federal programs/services with open-ended duration (until new agreement enters into force), unlike the rest of the CR which ends Nov 17, 2023.
All dollar amounts represent contract authority from the Airport and Airway Trust Fund (mandatory spending), not discretionary appropriations
All program extensions cover the period October 1 through December 31, 2023 (Q1 FY2024)
SEC. 2221 sets Counter-UAS authority to expire November 18, 2023, before the CR general expiration date

Title III as provided contains only Subtitle A (Animal Drug and Animal Generic Drug User Fee Amendments); Chapter 2 may be incomplete as it appears to be missing savings clause, effective date, and sunset date sections analogous to SEC. 2305-2307 in Chapter 1
Dollar amounts ($33,500,000 for ADUFA and $25,000,000 for AGDUFA) represent annual user fee revenue targets authorized to be collected from industry, not direct appropriations from the Treasury
AGDUFA fee revenue increased significantly from $18,336,340 to $25,000,000 per year
New generic investigational new animal drug file fee of $50,000 per file established in SEC. 2312(c)
SEC. 2342 reduces Medicaid Improvement Fund from $7,000,000,000 to $6,357,117,810, a net reduction of $642,882,190 serving as a budget offset
SEC. 2351 contains an indefinite appropriation ('such sums as may be necessary') with no specific dollar amount
SEC. 2352 and SEC. 2353 provide pro-rata funding for Oct 1 – Nov 17, 2023 but do not specify explicit dollar amounts in the text
All extensions in this title run through November 17, 2023, matching the duration of the continuing resolution in Division A
